skill.name: system-design
skill.description: Guides software architecture sessions using Clean Architecture, Hexagonal Architecture, domain modeling, ADRs, and TypeScript scaffold templates. It uses a Socratic workflow to help teams define requirements, model domains, set boundaries, and generate layered project structures.
skill.summary: Design Clean or Hexagonal Architecture systems with guided discovery, domain modeling, ADRs, and TypeScript scaffolds.
skill.author: AaronAbuUsama

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many command, filesystem, network, credential, and weak-crypto indicators, but review found these are Markdown documentation and TypeScript scaffold examples. No executable scripts, malicious prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or hidden runtime behavior were found in the reviewed files. Publishable with low risk because generated examples may still require normal user review before use in production.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Fence Patterns Misclassified as External Commands
low_findings.description: The external command findings are false positives caused by Markdown inline code and fenced examples. The reviewed files instruct the assistant to read workflow and template documents, and they show TypeScript examples rather than executing shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited sections are Markdown tables or code fences, not Ruby backtick execution. Multiple representative hits match documentation formatting rather than executable files.

low_findings.title: Relative Import Paths Misclassified as Filesystem Traversal
low_findings.description: The filesystem path traversal findings are false positives from TypeScript relative imports inside template examples. They demonstrate normal module imports such as domain and application layer references, not file reads or writes outside a sandbox.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The patterns occur in fenced TypeScript snippets and use static import syntax. No evidence shows runtime path construction, arbitrary file access, or traversal using user input.

low_findings.title: Network Indicators Are Documentation or Local Test URLs
low_findings.description: Network findings are low risk because they appear in architecture references, localhost test requests, or examples of outbound ports. The skill does not perform network requests by itself.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited network strings are a bad-practice example, a localhost Request used in tests, and public reference links. No evidence found of data exfiltration or automatic outbound calls.

low_findings.title: Environment Access Limited to Non-Secret Port Configuration
low_findings.description: The environment variable finding is a benign server template reading PORT for local configuration. It does not access credentials, tokens, or secret-bearing environment variables.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The only reviewed environment access is process.env.PORT with a numeric fallback. No evidence found of reading API keys, database URLs, or sending environment values elsewhere.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Semantic False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings appear to be triggered by architecture terminology, ordinary code examples, or crypto.randomUUID used for identifiers. No MD5, SHA1, encryption downgrade, or password hashing pattern was found in the reviewed evidence.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Representative hits do not show weak cryptographic primitives. crypto.randomUUID is an identifier generator in a domain object template, not a weak cryptographic algorithm.

content.value_statement: Complex software designs often fail because requirements, boundaries, and tradeoffs are unclear. This skill guides teams through discovery, domain modeling, boundary design, ADRs, and TypeScript scaffold creation.
actual_capabilities: Runs a four-phase workflow for discovery, domain modeling, boundary definition, and scaffolding.
Asks structured questions to clarify actors, constraints, success criteria, and failure modes.
Creates domain model outputs with entities, value objects, aggregates, relationships, and Mermaid diagrams.
Defines inbound and outbound ports with adapters for HTTP, persistence, external APIs, events, and notifications.
Provides TypeScript templates for entities, value objects, repositories, use cases, controllers, and ADRs.
Documents architecture decisions with context, options, tradeoffs, consequences, and follow-up steps.
limitations: It provides guidance and templates, not a full production system by itself.
Generated scaffolds require project-specific validation, testing, security review, and dependency choices.
It focuses on Clean and Hexagonal Architecture, so it may not fit every architectural style.
It cannot know current organizational constraints unless the user provides them during discovery.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Service Architecture
use_cases.description: Use the discovery and modeling phases to define the first reliable structure for a product or backend service.

use_cases.title: Refactor a Coupled Codebase
use_cases.description: Identify domain concepts, ports, adapters, and dependency boundaries before moving logic out of frameworks or controllers.

use_cases.title: Document Team Architecture Decisions
use_cases.description: Create ADRs that explain context, options, decisions, risks, and consequences for future maintainers.

prompt_templates.title: Start Architecture Discovery
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner: clarify a new system before choosing technologies.
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me architect a system for [problem]. Start with discovery questions before proposing a design.

prompt_templates.title: Model the Domain
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: turn requirements into domain concepts.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the modeling phase for [system]. Identify entities, value objects, aggregates, relationships, invariants, and open questions.

prompt_templates.title: Define Ports and Adapters
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate: design system boundaries after discovery.
prompt_templates.prompt: Given this domain model and requirements, define inbound ports, outbound ports, adapters, layer responsibilities, and dependency rules.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Scaffold and ADR
prompt_templates.prompt: Using these decisions, generate a TypeScript Clean Architecture scaffold plan and an ADR with context, options, decision, risks, and consequences.

output_examples.input: I need to design an order service for a marketplace.
output: A discovery summary with actors, constraints, success criteria, and unresolved questions.
A domain model with orders, customers, payments, inventory, value objects, and business invariants.
A boundary map that separates use cases, repositories, payment gateway, HTTP controllers, and event publishing.

output_examples.input: Refactor this controller-heavy API into cleaner layers.
output: A list of business rules that should move from controllers into use cases or entities.
Recommended ports for persistence, notification, and external services.
A migration plan that keeps adapters thin and protects the domain from framework details.

output_examples.input: Write an ADR for choosing Hexagonal Architecture.
output: An ADR with status, context, constraints, options, decision drivers, selected approach, consequences, and related decisions.
A clear explanation of why ports and adapters fit the project constraints.

best_practices: Complete discovery and domain modeling before asking for scaffold generation.
Provide real constraints, expected load, team experience, integrations, and failure modes.
Review generated templates for security, validation, testing, and operational requirements before implementation.
anti_patterns: Asking for a full scaffold before the problem, actors, and boundaries are clear.
Treating the template output as production-ready code without review or tests.
Forcing Hexagonal Architecture onto a small script or simple workflow that does not need layered design.

faq.question: Does this skill write production-ready code?
faq.answer: No. It creates architecture guidance and scaffold templates that need project-specific review, implementation, and testing.

faq.question: What architecture styles does it support best?
faq.answer: It is strongest for Clean Architecture, Hexagonal Architecture, ports and adapters, and domain-driven modeling.

faq.question: Can it help with existing systems?
faq.answer: Yes. It can analyze coupling, identify boundaries, and plan a staged refactor toward clearer layers.

faq.question: Does it require TypeScript?
faq.answer: The included scaffold templates are TypeScript-focused, but the discovery and design workflow can guide other languages.

faq.question: How does it make design decisions?
faq.answer: It asks questions, presents tradeoffs, and documents choices. The user remains responsible for final decisions.

faq.question: Is it safe to use with private architecture details?
faq.answer: Use normal confidentiality controls for your AI tool. The skill itself is documentation and does not include code that sends data elsewhere.
